title: California Unveils Around $1.45 Billion Boost for Zero-Emission Transportation and EV Infrastructure
content: Governor Gavin Newsom has announced a new funding to accelerate Californiaâ€™s shift to clean transportation, with a strong focus on zero- and low-emission vehicles and infrastructure. The investment includes $1.45 billion from SB 1 to support clean transportation solutions, including projects that connect ports to rail yards and reduce congestion and emissions along freight corridors. An additional $49 million will fund the development of medium- and heavy-duty truck charging hubs in Fresno, Oakland, Ontario, and San Diego. Another $28 million is allocated to build ultra-fast EV charging stations along major highways like Interstate 5 and State Route 99, boosting the stateâ€™s long-distance EV travel capacity.
author: Analysts Account
